originalTitle: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
title: A Special Story About Omar the Referee!
content:
Meet Omar Artan, Our Football Friend!

Hello, my friends! Today, we have a very special story about a kind man named Omar Artan. Omar lives in a country called Somalia, which is a beautiful place in Africa, just like where many of us live! Omar loves football, just like many of us do. He doesn't play the game like a striker scoring goals or a goalkeeper saving them. Instead, Omar has a very important job in football: he is a referee! Did you know? Somalia is a country in East Africa with a long coastline and sunny beaches! Many people there love football, just like you!

What Does a Referee Do?

Imagine you are playing football with your friends. Sometimes, someone might not play fair, right? Maybe they push, or touch the ball with their hand when they shouldn't. That's where a referee comes in! A referee is like a special judge on the football field. Their job is to make sure everyone follows the rules of the game. They blow a whistle if something is wrong, and they show cards to help players remember the rules. It's a very big job because they help make sure the game is fair and fun for everyone! Omar Artan is a very good referee, and he works hard to make sure games are fair. Did you know? The referee uses a whistle to stop the game and restart it. They also use yellow and red cards to warn players!

The Biggest Football Party: The World Cup!

Now, imagine the biggest football party in the whole wide world! This party is called the World Cup. It happens only once every four years! Teams from all over the world – from Africa, Europe, Asia, America – come together to play football and see who is the best. It's a very exciting time, with lots of cheers and amazing goals! Many boys and girls dream of playing in the World Cup one day. It's a huge honor for any player, and also for a referee! Omar Artan was chosen to be one of the referees for this very special World Cup! Did you know? The World Cup trophy is made of real gold! It’s what all the teams want to win!

Omar's Big Dream Trip

Omar Artan worked very, very hard to become such a good referee. He practiced and studied all the rules. Because he was so good, he got a special invitation! The people who organize the World Cup asked him to come and be a referee at the big games. This World Cup was happening in a country called the United States of America. So, Omar was getting ready to pack his bags, put on his referee uniform, and fly on a big airplane to America! He was going to help make sure the biggest football games in the world were fair. Imagine how happy and proud he must have been! Did you know? The United States of America is a very big country, far away from Africa! It takes many hours to fly there on a plane.

A Little Problem on the Way

But then, something unexpected happened. Omar was all ready to go, but he couldn't travel to the United States. It wasn't because he did anything wrong. It was because of some rules about people traveling to that country. Sometimes, countries have special rules about who can come in. So, even though Omar was a great referee and was invited, he wasn't allowed to get on the airplane to go to America. This must have been very sad for him. Imagine wanting to go to the biggest football party and then not being able to! Did you know? Sometimes, even if you have an invitation, you need a special paper called a “visa” to visit another country.

No Trip, But Still a Hero

Even though Omar couldn't travel to America and blow his whistle at the World Cup games, he was still a hero in our eyes. He worked so hard, and he was chosen because he is one of the best referees. It wasn't his fault that he couldn't go. He had a big dream, and even if it didn't come true in the way he expected, he still tried his very best. It's important to remember that sometimes things don't go as planned, but we should always be proud of trying our best. Did you know? There are over 250 million football players in the world! That's a lot of people who love the game!

Good News from Fifa!

Now for the really good news! There is a very important group called Fifa. Fifa is like the big boss of world football. They help organize the World Cup and make sure football is played all over the world. When Fifa heard that Omar couldn't go to the World Cup, they thought about what was fair. Even though he couldn't be there in person, he was chosen because he deserved it. So, Fifa decided something wonderful! Did you know? Fifa stands for "Fédération Internationale de Football Association." It's a French name!

What is a "Fee" Anyway?

When someone works hard at a job, they usually get paid, right? This money is called a "fee" or a "salary." It's what you get for doing your work. The referees who go to the World Cup get a special fee for their important job. Even though Omar couldn't fly to America, Fifa decided that he would still get his World Cup fee. Yes, that's right! He will get all the money he was supposed to get, even without going! Did you know? Many jobs have a "fee" or "salary" attached to them. It's how people earn money to buy food, clothes, and help their families.

Why This is Fair and Kind

This is such a kind and fair thing for Fifa to do! It shows that they appreciate Omar's hard work and talent. Even though he faced a problem that wasn't his fault, they still wanted to make sure he was rewarded. It teaches us that it's important to be fair to everyone, and to recognize people's efforts, even when things don't go perfectly. Omar Artan is a great example of someone who tried his best, and he is still being celebrated. It means a lot to him and to his country, Somalia. Did you know? Being fair means treating everyone equally and making sure things are just for all.

Dreams Can Still Shine Bright!

So, even though Omar Artan didn't get to stand on the big World Cup field this time, he still got his reward. This story reminds us that if we work hard and follow our dreams, good things can happen. And sometimes, even if there are bumps on the road, people will see your effort and be fair. Maybe next time, Omar will get to go to the World Cup and blow his whistle loud and clear! He is a champion referee, and his story makes us feel happy and hopeful!
summary: Omar Artan, a referee from Somalia who couldn't travel to the World Cup in America, will still get all his payment from Fifa because they want to be fair.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows that even if you face problems, working hard and being good at what you do can still lead to a fair reward, and big organizations like Fifa care about being kind.

originalTitle: Author Chimamanda Adichie accuses hospital of stalling review into son's death
title: A Mommy's Sad Story and Her Search for Answers
content:
Hello, friends! Today we have some news about a very special mommy from our own Africa, Nigeria. Her name is Chimamanda Adichie. She is a famous writer who tells amazing stories with words. But today, we share a sad story about her little baby boy, and how she is looking for answers.

Who is Chimamanda Adichie?

Imagine someone who is really good at telling stories, so good that people all over the world love to read her books! That's Chimamanda Adichie. She is from a beautiful country in Africa called Nigeria. She writes wonderful stories that make people think and feel. She is a very important person because her words help us learn about our world and each other. *Did you know?* Many famous writers come from Africa! They share amazing stories about our continent.

Her Little Boy

Chimamanda has a family, just like many of us. She had a sweet little baby boy. He was very young, just 21 months old. That means he was almost two years old, still a very tiny person, learning to talk and run and play. He was a very loved little boy, a bright light in his mommy's life. It's very sad when a baby passes away so young.

A Trip to the Hospital

One day, her baby boy got very, very sick. When we get sick, sometimes we need special help from doctors and nurses. So, his mommy took him to a hospital in a big city in Nigeria called Lagos. Hospitals are special places where kind doctors and clever nurses work very hard to help people feel better when they are not well. They use medicines and special tools to make sick people healthy again. *Did you know?* Lagos is one of the biggest cities in Africa! It's full of busy people and many exciting things.

Sad News

Even with all the help from the doctors and nurses, Chimamanda's baby boy did not get better. Sadly, he passed away at the hospital. This means he went to sleep forever. It was a very, very sad day for his mommy, his daddy, and everyone who loved him. When someone you love passes away, especially a little baby, it makes everyone feel very, very sad. It's okay to feel sad and to miss them.

Why Mommy Wants Answers

When something so sad happens, especially to a little baby, mommies and daddies want to know why. They want to understand exactly what happened. Chimamanda wants to know why her little boy passed away. She wants to know if everything that could be done was done. She wants a special look into everything that happened when her baby was in the hospital. This special look is like asking a lot of questions and looking at all the facts, like a puzzle. *Did you know?* Doctors and nurses work very hard to keep us healthy. Sometimes, even with all their hard work, sad things still happen.

Asking Lots of Questions

Mommy Chimamanda says that she feels the hospital is making it hard for her to get these answers. She feels they are being slow and not helping her to find out what happened quickly. She wants them to help her understand every step of what happened to her dear baby boy. She wants them to really look into it, so she can have peace in her heart and understand. It's like when you lose your favorite toy, and you ask everyone where it went, because you really want to find it. She wants to find the answers to why her baby passed away.

Why This Is Important

It's very important for mommies and daddies to get answers when their child passes away. It helps them to understand and to heal. It also helps hospitals to learn new things and make sure that other children can get the very best care. When we ask questions and learn, we can make things better for everyone. It helps to make sure that hospitals are safe and strong places for all children who need help. Chimamanda is being very brave by asking these questions, even when she is very sad. *Did you know?* Asking questions is a super important way to learn! It helps us understand the world around us.

A Mommy's Hope

We hope that Mommy Chimamanda gets all the answers she is looking for. We hope that the hospital helps her to understand everything, so she can find peace. It's a very hard time for her and her family. We send her lots of love and strength from all of us here in Africa. Remember to always be kind to those who are sad, and to wish them well. We hope this story helps us all to understand why it's important to care for each other and to always seek the truth.
summary: Famous Nigerian writer Chimamanda Adichie is looking for answers about why her sweet 21-month-old baby boy passed away in a hospital in Lagos.
whyMatters: This is important because all mommies and daddies deserve to know what happened when a loved one gets sick, and it helps hospitals learn to keep everyone safe.

originalTitle: England-Croatia referee was called Grade E by Tuchel
title: England-Croatia referee was called Grade E by Tuchel
content: Clement Turpin is appointed referee for England's opening World Cup game against Croatia on Wednesday - and was once labelled a "Grade E" official by head coach Thomas Tuchel.

originalTitle: The 20-year-old who stole the show on Scotland's World Cup return
title: The 20-year-old who stole the show on Scotland's World Cup return
content: Ben Gannon-Doak wasn't born when Scotland last played at the men's World Cup. But the 20-year-old was at the heart of their long-awaited return and their first win at the tournament in 36 years as he stole the show against Haiti.
